getUnmatchedAlerts,\n18\t  parseAlerts,\n19\t  toStationAlert,\n20\t} from \"./alerts-parser.js\";\n21\timport {\n22\t  recordAlertsChange,\n23\t  recordFeedError,\n24\t  recordFeedPollDuration,\n25\t \"./observability/tracing.js\";\n30\t\n31\tconst POLL_INTERVAL_MS = POLLING_INTERVALS.alerts * 1000; // 60,000 ms\n32\tconst FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS = 15_000;\n33\tconst CIRCUIT_OPEN_AFTER = 3;\n34\tconst CIRCUIT_RESET_MS = 60_000;\n35\t\n36\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n37\t// Types\n38\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n39\t\n40\texport interface AlertChange {\n41\t  /** Type of change */\n42\t  type: \"new\" | \"updated\" | \"resolved\";\n43\t  /** The alert (for new/updated) or the alert that was resolved */\n44\t  alert: StationAlert;\n45\t  /** Timestamp when this change was detected */\n46\t  detectedAt: number;\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\texport interface AlertsCache {\n50\t  /** All current alerts */\n51\t  alerts: ParsedAlert[];\n52\t  /** Timestamp when alerts were last fetched */\n53\t  lastFetchAt: number | null;\n54\t  /** Timestamp when alerts were last successfully parsed */\n55\t  lastSuccessAt: number | null;\n56\t  /** Pattern match rate (0-1) */\n57\t  matchRate: number;\n58\t  /** Number of consecutive fetch failures */\n59\t  consecutiveFailures: number;\n60\t  /** Whether the circuit breaker is open */\n61\t  circuitOpen: boolean;\n62\t  /** When the circuit was opened (null if closed) */\n63\t  circuitOpenAt: number | null;\n64\t}\n65\t\n66\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n67\t// Module state\n68\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n69\t\n70\tconst cache: AlertsCache = {\n71\t  alerts: [],\n72\t  lastFetchAt: null,\n73\t  lastSuccessAt: null,\n74\t  matchRate: 0,\n75\t  consecutiveFailures: 0,\n76\t  circuitOpen: false,\n77\t  circuitOpenAt: null,\n78\t};\n79\t\n80\tlet pollTimer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;\n81\tlet previousAlertIds = new Set<string>();\n82\t\n83\t/** Listeners for alert changes (for push notifications) */\n84\tconst changeListeners: Array<(changes: AlertChange[]) => void> = [];\n85\t\n86\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n87\t// Alert fetching\n88\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n89\t\n90\t/**\n91\t * Fetch and parse the alerts feed.\n92\t */\n93\tasync function fetchAlerts(): Promise<ParsedAlert[] | null> {\n94\t  // Check circuit breaker\n95\t  if (cache.circuitOpen && cache.circuitOpenAt) {\n96\t    if (Date.now() - cache.circuitOpenAt >= CIRCUIT_RESET_MS) {\n97\t      // Reset circuit\n98\t      cache.circuitOpen = false;\n99\t      cache.circuitOpenAt = null;\n100\t      cache.consecutiveFailures = 0;\n101\t    } else {\n102\t      logger.warn(\"Alerts circuit breaker is open\");\n103\t      return null;\n104\t    }\n105\t  }\n106\t\n107\t  const start = Date.now();\n108\t  const headers: Record<string, string> = {\n109\t    Accept: \"application/x-protobuf\",\n110\t  };\n111\t\n112\t  try {\n113\t    const response = await tracedFetch(MTA_ALERTS_FEED_URL, {\n114\t      method: \"GET\",\n115\t      headers,\n116\t      signal: AbortSignal.timeout(FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS),\n117\t      spanName: \"MTA Alerts Feed\",\n118\t    });\n119\t\n120\t    if (!response.ok) {\n121\t      throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`);\n122\t    }\n123\t\n124\t    const buffer = await response.arrayBuffer();\n125\t    const data = new Uint8Array(buffer);\n126\t    const alerts = await withChildSpan(\"parse-alerts\", () => parseAlerts(data), {\n127\t      \"alert.count\": data.length,\n128\t    });\n129\t\n130\t    // Success - reset failure count\n131\t    cache.consecutiveFailures = 0;\n132\t    cache.circuitOpen = false;\n133\t    cache.circuitOpenAt = null;\n134\t    cache.lastSuccessAt = Date.now();\n135\t    cache.matchRate = calculateMatchRate(alerts);\n136\t\n137\t    const latencyMs = Date.now() - start;\n138\t\n139\t    // Record alerts feed poll duration metric\n140\t    recordFeedPollDuration(latencyMs / 1000, \"alerts\");\n141\t\n142\t    const matchedCount = alerts.filter((a) => a.patternMatched).length;\n143\t\n144\t    // Update alert metrics\n145\t    setAlertsActive(alerts.length);\n146\t    setAlertsMatchRate(cache.matchRate);\n147\t\n148\t    logger.info(\"Alerts fetched successfully\", {\n149\t      latency_ms: latencyMs,\n150\t      alert_count: alerts.length,\n151\t      match_rate: Math.round(cache.matchRate * 100) / 100,\n152\t      matched_count: matchedCount,\n153\t      unmatched_count: alerts.length - matchedCount,\n154\t    });\n155\t\n156\t    return alerts;\n157\t  } catch (err) {\n158\t    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n159\t    const latencyMs = Date.now() - start;\n160\t    cache.consecutiveFailures++;\n161\t\n162\t    if (cache.consecutiveFailures >= CIRCUIT_OPEN_AFTER && !cache.circuitOpen) {\n163\t      cache.circuitOpen = true;\n164\t      cache.circuitOpenAt = Date.now();\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    // Record alerts feed poll duration and error metrics\n168\t    recordFeedPollDuration(latencyMs / 1000, \"alerts\");\n169\t\n170\t    // Determine error type for metric labeling\n171\t    let errorType: string;\n172\t    if (err instanceof Error) {\n173\t      if (err.name === \"AbortError\") {\n174\t        errorType = \"timeout\";\n175\t      } else if (err.name === \"TypeError\") {\n176\t        errorType = \"network\";\n177\t      } else if (\"status\" in err) {\n178\t        const status = (err as { status: number }).status;\n179\t        errorType = status === 429 ? \"rate_limited\" : status >= 500 ? \"server_error\" : \"http_error\";\n180\t      } else {\n181\t        errorType = \"unknown\";\n182\t      }\n183\t    } else {\n184\t      errorType = \"unknown\";\n185\t    }\n186\t    recordFeedError(\"alerts\", errorType);\n187\t\n188\t    logger.error(\"Alerts fetch failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n189\t      latency_ms: latencyMs,\n190\t      error: message,\n191\t      consecutive_failures: cache.consecutiveFailures,\n192\t      circuit_open: cache.circuitOpen,\n193\t    });\n194\t\n195\t    return null;\n196\t  }\n197\t}\n198\t\n199\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n200\t// Alert diffing\n201\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n202\t\n203\t/**\n204\t * Compare new alerts with previous state to detect changes.\n205\t */\n206\tfunction detectChanges(newAlerts: ParsedAlert[]): AlertChange[] {\n207\t  const changes: AlertChange[] = [];\n208\t  const now = Date.now();\n209\t  const newAlertIds = new Set(newAlerts.map((a) => a.id));\n210\t\n211\t  // Detect new and updated alerts\n212\t  for (const alert of newAlerts) {\n213\t    if (!previousAlertIds.has(alert.id)) {\n214\t      // New alert\n215\t      changes.push({\n216\t        type: \"new\",\n217\t        alert: toStationAlert(alert),\n218\t        detectedAt: now,\n219\t      });\n220\t    } else {\n221\t      // Check if updated (by comparing modifiedAt)\n222\t      const prevAlert = cache.alerts.find((a) => a.id === alert.id);\n223\t      if (prevAlert && alert.modifiedAt > prevAlert.modifiedAt) {\n224\t        changes.push({\n225\t          type: \"updated\",\n226\t          alert: toStationAlert(alert),\n227\t          detectedAt: now,\n228\t        });\n229\t      }\n230\t    }\n231\t  }\n232\t\n233\t  // Detect resolved alerts\n234\t  for (const prevAlert of cache.alerts) {\n235\t    if (!newAlertIds.has(prevAlert.id)) {\n236\t      changes.push({\n237\t        type: \"resolved\",\n238\t        alert: * Alerts poller: fetches MTA subway alerts feed every 60 seconds.\n *\n * Responsibilities:\n * - Poll the MTA alerts GTFS-RT feed on a 60-second interval\n * - Parse alerts using the alerts-parser module\n * - Track alert changes (new, updated, resolved) for push notifications\n * - Cache parsed alerts for API routes\n * - Circuit breaker for resilience\n */\n\nimport { MTA_ALERTS_FEED_URL, POLLING_INTERVALS } from \"@mta-my-way/shared\";\nimport type { StationAlert } from \"@mta-my-way/shared\";\nimport type { ParsedAlert } from \"./alerts-parser.js\";\nimport {\n  calculateMatchRate,\n  getUnmatchedAlerts,\n  parseAlerts,\n  toStationAlert,\n} from \"./alerts-parser.js\";\nimport {\n  recordAlertsChange,\n  recordFeedError,\n  recordFeedPollDuration,\n  setAlertsActive,\n  setAlertsMatchRate,\n} from \"./middleware/metrics.js\";\nimport { logger } from \"./observability/logger.js\";\nimport { tracedFetch, withChildSpan } from \"./observability/tracing.js\";\n\nconst POLL_INTERVAL_MS = POLLING_INTERVALS.alerts * 1000; // 60,000 ms\nconst FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS = 15_000;\nconst CIRCUIT_OPEN_AFTER = 3;\nconst CIRCUIT_RESET_MS = 60_000;\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Types\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nexport interface AlertChange {\n  /** Type of change */\n  type: \"new\" | \"updated\" | \"resolved\";\n  /** The alert (for new/updated) or the alert that was resolved */\n  alert: StationAlert;\n  /** Timestamp when this change was detected */\n  detectedAt: number;\n}\n\nexport interface AlertsCache {\n  /** All current alerts */\n  alerts: ParsedAlert[];\n  /** Timestamp when alerts were last fetched */\n  lastFetchAt: number | null;\n  /** Timestamp when alerts were last successfully parsed */\n  lastSuccessAt: number | null;\n  /** Pattern match rate (0-1) */\n  matchRate: number;\n  /** Number of consecutive fetch failures */\n  consecutiveFailures: number;\n  /** Whether the circuit breaker is open */\n  circuitOpen: boolean;\n  /** When the circuit was opened (null if closed) */\n  circuitOpenAt: number | null;\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Module state\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nconst cache: AlertsCache = {\n  alerts: [],\n  lastFetchAt: null,\n  lastSuccessAt: null,\n  matchRate: 0,\n  consecutiveFailures: 0,\n  circuitOpen: false,\n  circuitOpenAt: null,\n};\n\nlet pollTimer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;\nlet previousAlertIds = new Set<string>();\n\n/** Listeners for alert changes (for push notifications) */\nconst changeListeners: Array<(changes: AlertChange[]) => void> = [];\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Alert fetching\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Fetch and parse the alerts feed.\n */\nasync function fetchAlerts(): Promise<ParsedAlert[] | null> {\n  // Check circuit breaker\n  if (cache.circuitOpen && cache.circuitOpenAt) {\n    if (Date.now() - cache.circuitOpenAt >= CIRCUIT_RESET_MS) {\n      // Reset circuit\n      cache.circuitOpen = false;\n      cache.circuitOpenAt = null;\n      cache.consecutiveFailures = 0;\n    } else {\n      logger.warn(\"Alerts circuit breaker is open\");\n      return null;\n    }\n  }\n\n  const start = Date.now();\n  const headers: Record<string, string> = {\n    Accept: \"application/x-protobuf\",\n  };\n\n  try {\n    const response = await tracedFetch(MTA_ALERTS_FEED_URL, {\n      method: \"GET\",\n      headers,\n      signal: AbortSignal.timeout(FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS),\n      spanName: \"MTA Alerts Feed\",\n    });\n\n    if (!response.ok) {\n      throw new Error(`HTTP ${response.status} ${response.statusText}`);\n    }\n\n    const buffer = await response.arrayBuffer();\n    const data = new Uint8Array(buffer);\n    const alerts = await withChildSpan(\"parse-alerts\", () => parseAlerts(data), {\n      \"alert.count\": data.length,\n    });\n\n    // Success - reset failure count\n    cache.consecutiveFailures = 0;\n    cache.circuitOpen = false;\n    cache.circuitOpenAt = null;\n    cache.lastSuccessAt = Date.now();\n    cache.matchRate = calculateMatchRate(alerts);\n\n    const latencyMs = Date.now() - start;\n\n    // Record alerts feed poll duration metric\n    recordFeedPollDuration(latencyMs / 1000, \"alerts\");\n\n    const matchedCount = alerts.filter((a) => a.patternMatched).length;\n\n    // Update alert metrics\n    setAlertsActive(alerts.length);\n    setAlertsMatchRate(cache.matchRate);\n\n    logger.info(\"Alerts fetched successfully\", {\n      latency_ms: latencyMs,\n      alert_count: alerts.length,\n      match_rate: Math.round(cache.matchRate * 100) / 100,\n      matched_count: matchedCount,\n      unmatched_count: alerts.length - matchedCount,\n    });\n\n    return alerts;\n  } catch (err) {\n    const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);\n    const latencyMs = Date.now() - start;\n    cache.consecutiveFailures++;\n\n    if (cache.consecutiveFailures >= CIRCUIT_OPEN_AFTER && !cache.circuitOpen) {\n      cache.circuitOpen = true;\n      cache.circuitOpenAt = Date.now();\n    }\n\n    // Record alerts feed poll duration and error metrics\n    recordFeedPollDuration(latencyMs / 1000, \"alerts\");\n\n    // Determine error type for metric labeling\n    let errorType: string;\n    if (err instanceof Error) {\n      if (err.name === \"AbortError\") {\n        errorType = \"timeout\";\n      } else if (err.name === \"TypeError\") {\n        errorType = \"network\";\n      } else if (\"status\" in err) {\n        const status = (err as { status: number }).status;\n        errorType = status === 429 ? \"rate_limited\" : status >= 500 ? \"server_error\" : \"http_error\";\n      } else {\n        errorType = \"unknown\";\n      }\n    } else {\n      errorType = \"unknown\";\n    }\n    recordFeedError(\"alerts\", errorType);\n\n    logger.error(\"Alerts fetch failed\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n      latency_ms: latencyMs,\n      error: message,\n      consecutive_failures: cache.consecutiveFailures,\n      circuit_open: cache.circuitOpen,\n    });\n\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Alert diffing\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Compare new alerts with previous state to detect changes.\n */\nfunction detectChanges(newAlerts: ParsedAlert[]): AlertChange[] {\n  const changes: AlertChange[] = [];\n  const now = Date.now();\n  const newAlertIds = new Set(newAlerts.map((a) => a.id));\n\n  // Detect new and updated alerts\n  for (const alert of newAlerts) {\n    if (!previousAlertIds.has(alert.id)) {\n      // New alert\n      changes.push({\n        type: \"new\",\n        alert: toStationAlert(alert),\n        detectedAt: now,\n      });\n    } else {\n      // Check if updated (by comparing modifiedAt)\n      const prevAlert = cache.alerts.find((a) => a.id === alert.id);\n      if (prevAlert && alert.modifiedAt > prevAlert.modifiedAt) {\n        changes.push({\n          type: \"updated\",\n          alert: toStationAlert(alert),\n          detectedAt: now,\n        });\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Detect resolved alerts\n  for (const prevAlert of cache.alerts) {\n    if (!newAlertIds.has(prevAlert.id)) {\n      changes.push({\n        type: \"resolved\",\n        alert: toStationAlert(prevAlert),\n        detectedAt: now,\n      });\n    }\n  }\n\n  return changes;\n}\n\n/**\n * Notify all change listeners.\n */\nfunction notifyListeners(changes: AlertChange[]): void {\n  if (changes.length === 0) return;\n\n  for (const listener of changeListeners) {\n    try {\n      listener(changes);\n    } catch (err) {\n      logger.error(\"Alert change listener error\", err instanceof Error ? err : undefined, {\n        error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),\n      });\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Poll cycle\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\nasync function runPoll(): Promise<void> {\n  cache.lastFetchAt = Date.now();\n\n  const alerts = await fetchAlerts();\n  if (alerts === null) {\n    // Fetch failed, keep existing cache\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Detect changes before updating cache\n  const changes = detectChanges(alerts);\n\n  // Update cache\n  const previousIds = new Set(cache.alerts.map((a) => a.id));\n  previousAlertIds = previousIds;\n  cache.alerts = alerts;\n\n  // Log changes\n  if (changes.length > 0) {\n    const newCount = changes.filter((c) => c.type === \"new\").length;\n    const updatedCount = changes.filter((c) => c.type === \"updated\").length;\n    const resolvedCount = changes.filter((c) => c.type === \"resolved\").length;\n\n    logger.info(\"Alerts changed\", {\n      new: newCount,\n      updated: updatedCount,\n      resolved: resolvedCount,\n    });\n\n    // Record alert change metrics\n    if (newCount > 0) recordAlertsChange(\"new\");\n    if (updatedCount > 0) recordAlertsChange(\"updated\");\n    if (resolvedCount > 0) recordAlertsChange(\"resolved\");\n\n    // Notify listeners\n    notifyListeners(changes);\n  }\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Public API\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Start the alerts polling loop.\n * First poll fires immediately.\n */\nexport function startAlertsPoller(): void {\n  void runPoll();\n  pollTimer = setInterval(() => void runPoll(), POLL_INTERVAL_MS);\n\n  logger.info(\"Alerts poller started\", {\n    interval_ms: POLL_INTERVAL_MS,\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Stop the alerts poller.\n */\nexport function stopAlertsPoller(): void {\n  if (pollTimer !== null) {\n    clearInterval(pollTimer);\n    pollTimer = null;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Get all current alerts.\n */\nexport function getAllAlerts(): StationAlert[] {\n  return cache.alerts.map(toStationAlert);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get alerts filtered by line ID.\n */\nexport function getAlertsForLine(lineId: string): StationAlert[] {\n  return cache.alerts.filter((a) => a.affectedLines.includes(lineId)).map(toStationAlert);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get alerts filtered by multiple line IDs.\n */\nexport function getAlertsForLines(lineIds: string[]): StationAlert[] {\n  return cache.alerts\n    .filter((a) => a.affectedLines.some((line: string) => lineIds.includes(line)))\n    .map(toStationAlert);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get alerts affecting a specific station.\n * Note: This requires station IDs to be extracted from the alert.\n */\nexport function getAlertsForStation(stationId: string): StationAlert[] {\n  return cache.alerts.filter((a) => a.affectedStations.includes(stationId)).map(toStationAlert);\n}\n\n/**\n * Get the alerts cache status for the health endpoint.\n */\nexport function getAlertsStatus(): {\n  lastFetchAt: string | null;\n  lastSuccessAt: string | null;\n  alertCount: number;\n  matchRate: number;\n  consecutiveFailures: number;\n  circuitOpen: boolean;\n  unmatchedCount: number;\n} {\n  return {\n    lastFetchAt: cache.lastFetchAt ? new Date(cache.lastFetchAt).toISOString() : null,\n    lastSuccessAt: cache.lastSuccessAt ? new Date(cache.lastSuccessAt).toISOString() : null,\n    alertCount: cache.alerts.length,\n    matchRate: cache.matchRate,\n    consecutiveFailures: cache.consecutiveFailures,\n    circuitOpen: cache.circuitOpen,\n    unmatchedCount: getUnmatchedAlerts().length,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Get alerts feed age in seconds.\n */\nexport function getAlertsAgeSeconds(): number {\n  if (!cache.lastSuccessAt) return 0;\n  return Math.floor((Date.now() - cache.lastSuccessAt) / 1000);\n}\n\n/**\n * Register a listener for alert changes.\n * Returns an unsubscribe function.\n */\nexport function onAlertChange(listener: (changes: AlertChange[]) => void): () => void {\n  changeListeners.push(listener);\n  return () => {\n    const index = changeListeners.indexOf(listener);\n    if (index >= 0) {\n      changeListeners.splice(index, 1);\n    }\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Force a refresh of the alerts feed.\n */\nexport async function refreshAlerts(): Promise<StationAlert[]> {\n  await runPoll();\n  return getAllAlerts();\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// Testing helpers (only used in integration tests)\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Set alerts for testing.\n * This helper is only used in integration tests to set up test data.\n */\nexport function setAlertsForTesting(alerts: ParsedAlert[]): void {\n  cache.alerts = alerts;\n  cache.lastSuccessAt = Date.now();\n  cache.lastFetchAt = Date.now();\n  cache.matchRate = 1.0;\n  cache.consecutiveFailures = 0;\n  cache.circuitOpen = false;\n}\n\n/**\n * Reset alerts cache to initial state.\n * This helper is only used in tests to reset module state.\n */\nexport function resetAlertsCacheForTesting(): void {\n  if (pollTimer !== null) {\n    clearInterval(pollTimer);\n    pollTimer = null;\n  }\n  cache.alerts = [];\n  cache.lastFetchAt = null;\n  cache.lastSuccessAt = null;\n  cache.matchRate = 0;\n  cache.consecutiveFailures = 0;\n  cache.circuitOpen = false;\n  cache.circuitOpenAt = null;\n  previousAlertIds.clear();\n  changeListeners.length = 0;\n}\n","numLines":454,"startLine":1,"totalLines":454}}}

*/\nexport async function resetAllModuleState(): Promise<void> {\n  await cleanupAllState();\n}\n\n/**\n * Comprehensive cleanup of ALL shared mutable state across the server package.\n *\n * Call this in a `beforeEach` hook (or at the start of any test that needs\n * isolation) so that no module-level Map / Set / array leaks from a\n * previous test into the current one.\n *\n * Each step is guarded so that individual test files which `vi.mock()` a\n * module (replacing its exports with stubs) will not cause the entire\n * cleanup to blow up — the missing reset function is simply skipped.\n */\nexport async function cleanupAllState(): Promise<void> {\n  // Helper: call `fn` only if it exists on the imported module.\n  const tryReset = async (spec: string, ...fns: string[]): Promise<void> => {\n    try {\n      const mod = await import(spec);\n      for (const fn of fns) {\n        if (typeof mod[fn] === \"function\") {\n          await mod[fn]();\n        }\n      }\n    } catch {\n      // Module may be mocked or not reachable — skip silently.\n    }\n  };\n\n  // ---- cache.ts ----------------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../cache.js\", \"resetAllCacheStateForTesting\");\n\n  // ---- alerts-poller.ts ---------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../alerts-poller.js\", \"resetAlertsCacheForTesting\");\n\n  // ---- authentication.ts -------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../middleware/authentication.js\", \"resetAuthenticationState\");\n\n  // ---- api-key-management.ts ---------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../middleware/api-key-management.js\", \"clearAllApiKeys\");\n\n  // ---- rate-limiter.ts ---------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../middleware/rate-limiter.js\", \"resetRateLimiter\");\n\n  // ---- auth-rate-limit.ts ------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../middleware/auth-rate-limit.js\", \"_clearAllRateLimits\");\n\n  // ---- authorization-security.ts ------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../middleware/authorization-security.js\", \"clearAccessPatterns\");\n\n  // ---- audit-log.ts -------------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../middleware/audit-log.js\", \"resetAuditLog\");\n\n  // ---- token-encryption.ts ------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../middleware/token-encryption.js\", \"resetEncryptionState\");\n\n  // ---- trip-tracking.ts ---------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../trip-tracking.js\", \"resetTripTrackingForTesting\");\n\n  // ---- shuttle-matcher.ts -------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../shuttle-matcher.js\", \"resetShuttleCache\");\n\n  // ---- delay-detector.ts -------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../delay-detector.js\", \"resetDelayDetector\");\n\n  // ---- transformer.ts ------------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../transformer.js\", \"resetTransformerState\");\n\n  // ---- context-service.ts -------------------------------------------------\n  await tryReset(\"../context-service.js\", \"resetContextService\");\n}\n\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n// CSRF helpers for API tests\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n/**\n * Helper to get a CSRF token from the test app.\n * Makes a GET request to /api/csrf-token and extracts the token.\n */\nexport async function getCsrfToken(app: {\n  request(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<Response>;\n}): Promise<string> {\n  const res = await app.request(\"/api/csrf-token\");\n  if (res.status !== 200) {\n    throw new Error(`Failed to get CSRF token: ${res.status}`);\n  }\n  const body = (await res.json()) as { token: string };\n  return body.token;\n}\n\n/**\n * Helper to make a state-changing request with CSRF token.\n * Gets a fresh token and includes it in the X-CSRF-Token header.\n * Also merges in any provided authentication headers.\n */\nexport async function requestWithCsrf(\n  app: { request(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<Response> },\n  path: string,\n  options: RequestInit = {}\n): Promise<Response> {\n  const token = await getCsrfToken(app);\n  return app.request(path, {\n    ...options,\n    headers: {\n      ...options.headers,\n      \"X-CSRF-Token\": token,\n    },\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Helper to make an authenticated state-changing request with CSRF token.\n * Combines API key authentication with CSRF protection.\n */\nexport async function requestWithAuthAndCsrf(\n  app: { request(path: string, init?: RequestInit): Promise<Response> },\n  path: string,\n  authHeaders: { Authorization: string },\n  options: RequestInit = {}\n): Promise<Response> {\n  const token = await getCsrfToken(app);\n  return app.request(path, {\n    ...options,\n    headers: {\n      ...authHeaders,\n      ...options.headers,\n      \"X-CSRF-Token\": token,\n    },\n  });\n}\n","numLines":562,"startLine":1,"totalLines":562}}}
